text.title: api-monitoring-metrics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for api-monitoring-metrics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using user_id, request_id, or session_id as Prometheus metric labels.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates millions of time series. High-cardinality identifiers belong in logs and traces where per-event storage is expected, not in metrics where each unique label combination is a persistent time series consuming RAM.

failure-mode.description: Using default Prometheus histogram buckets (0.005s to 10s) for a service with sub-100ms p99 target.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Default coarse buckets give inaccurate percentile estimates at the tail. histogram_quantile(0.99) returns values that can be off by 10x. Tune buckets to your latency distribution or use native histograms (Prometheus 2.40+).

failure-mode.description: Disabling or skipping the metrics middleware for authenticated endpoints to avoid "overhead".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Missing metrics for authenticated routes hides 401/403 error rates and real traffic patterns on the most important endpoints. Middleware overhead is sub-millisecond and worth the full coverage.
